claims.claim: Risk-on: long-term conviction, best environment ever (regulatory clarity, $1T+ stablecoin volume, infra ready)
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Heavy new emphasis on 'credible neutrality' as the fundamental purpose of blockchains (multiple late-May replies) — building decentralization for shared infrastructure mutually distrustful parties can trust, not for its own sake
Announced/closed a $2.2B Crypto Fund 5 and framed near-all-time-low sentiment vs all-time-high fundamentals as the moment to lean in
New $75M investment in the ARC token (Circle's Arc institutional chain) — a specific stablecoin-infrastructure / configurable-privacy bet not in the prediction
Newly highlighted onchain capital markets: prediction markets, perpetual futures, onchain lending/credit, and tokenized real-world assets
Emphasis on institutional adoption and pragmatic, non-ideological founders ('collared shirt era') replacing cypherpunk roots
Strong support for specific US legislation (CLARITY Act, GENIUS Act) as the regulatory-clarity unlock
Cited Jolt / zero-knowledge proving breakthroughs and Jevons paradox framing for blockspace demand
notes: Window statements (Apr–Jun 2026) strongly reaffirm his core theses — privacy as the only moat (explicitly framed as private money + programmable private computation), stablecoins/payments, AI×crypto including AI agents, and risk-on long-term conviction (Fund 5, 'it's time to build'). Token-specific calls (ETH, EIGEN, ZRO, ALEO) and the explicit anti-undifferentiated-L1 concern were not revisited by name, hence absent; SOL is confirmed via his Solana/Consensus enthusiasm and the memecoin/anti-speculation stance is confirmed at the stance level ('pendulum swung too far,' fundamentals over hype). No contradictions found.
